# feat(ml): add backend/device/precision resolver for kailash-ml 2.0

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `2374577ff42e` — feat(ml): add backend/device/precision resolver for kailash-ml 2.0

**Body**:

Implements specs/ml-backends.md §2 (priority resolver), §3 (precision
auto-selection), §8 (typed error hierarchy) as Phase 2 of the
kailash-ml 2.0 rewrite.

Single entry: detect_backend(prefer=None) -> BackendInfo.

Priority order cuda -> mps -> rocm -> xpu -> tpu -> cpu (§2.2 MUST).
Explicit prefer= raises BackendUnavailable on miss with actionable
install hint and detected_backends list (§2.3 MUST, §8.2 MUST).
resolve_precision(info, "auto") maps per §3.2 device-capability table;
explicit precision requests are validated and raise
PrecisionUnsupported rather than silently downgrade (§3.3 MUST).

Graceful imports: torch / torch_xla probes live inside try/except so
a base install (no DL extras) still yields BackendInfo(backend="cpu").
No ImportError escapes to the caller.

Error hierarchy: BackendError base + BackendUnavailable,
UnsupportedFamily, PrecisionUnsupported carrying the fields §8.2
requires (requested, detected_backends, install_hint,
diagnostic_source; requested_precision, cuda_capability,
suggested_precision, etc).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
